creator: nbe@renzel.net
text:
Created attachment 144679
linphone coredump

Hi,

latest "linphone 3.7.0" segfaults on startup:
===============================================================================
** (linphone:60122): WARNING **: Couldn't find pixmap file: linphone.png

(linphone:60122): Gtk-WARNING **: Could not find signal handler 'linphone_gtk_internet_kind_changed'
belle-sip-warning-There is no object pool created in thread [34624005120]. Use belle_sip_object_pool_push() to create one. Unowned objects not unref'd will be leaked.
Segmentation fault
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
(gdb) bt
#0  0x0000000806023ca0 in memmove () from /lib/libc.so.7
#1  0x000000080cacd9fd in antlr3StringFactoryNew () from /usr/local/lib/libantlr3c.so
#2  0x000000080cacf509 in antlr3CommonTokenStreamNew () from /usr/local/lib/libantlr3c.so
#3  0x0000000805b2ff7f in belle_sip_messageParserNewSSD () from /usr/local/lib/libbellesip.so.0
#4  0x0000000805b2f77f in belle_sip_messageParserNewSSD () from /usr/local/lib/libbellesip.so.0
#5  0x0000000805b23d35 in belle_sip_messageParserNewSSD () from /usr/local/lib/libbellesip.so.0
#6  0x0000000805b23a1a in belle_sip_messageParserNewSSD () from /usr/local/lib/libbellesip.so.0
#7  0x0000000805b23937 in belle_sip_messageParserNewSSD () from /usr/local/lib/libbellesip.so.0
#8  0x0000000805ae1710 in belle_sip_header_address_parse () from /usr/local/lib/libbellesip.so.0
#9  0x00000008017c8630 in sal_address_new () from /usr/local/lib/liblinphone.so.6
#10 0x00000008017ad51f in linphone_address_new () from /usr/local/lib/liblinphone.so.6
#11 0x000000080179d638 in linphone_core_set_primary_contact () from /usr/local/lib/liblinphone.so.6
#12 0x000000080179c1ec in linphone_configuring_terminated () from /usr/local/lib/liblinphone.so.6
#13 0x000000080179d5ed in linphone_core_new_with_config () from /usr/local/lib/liblinphone.so.6
#14 0x000000000041b201 in main ()
===============================================================================

I'm running "FreeBSD 10.0-STABLE r268604M (amd64)"...


Regards,
Nils
